title: Separate Groups or Column Hiding
excerpt: Is there a way to separate the groups from each other so they can have their own columns? If not, is there a way to hide columns on specific groups so they can appear to have their own columns? If not, can we get one of …

posts.author: KelliATB (Kelli Van Voorhis)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there a way to separate the groups from each other so they can have their own columns? If not, is there a way to hide columns on specific groups so they can appear to have their own columns? If not, can we get one of these options please?

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @KelliATB - and welcome to the community.

At this time within a board you cannot change/hide columns within specific groups. All changes made apply to all groups within the board.

posts.author: Randi1 (Randi Fuchs)
posts.cookedHtml:
Agreed with @KelliATB, this would be really helpful!

Often times, columns do not apply to all the groups in the board and causes a lot of excess columns for the whole board.

Is this on the roadmap?

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Randi1 - I have not heard of this on the roadmap and it would offer a lot of complexity to implement. Having groups within a board stay the same adds continuity for reporting which would be lost with having varying group designs per board.

If you have significantly variation in board designs it always best to split into different boards. There are also creative ways around this with mirroring of fields, etc. from other boards, but this of course is dependent on your use case.

We are always more than happy to discuss design approaches in complementary sessions if you are interested. If so, feel free to reach out to me here and also check us out online!

Take care,
Mark

Green Llamas

monday.com + Green Llamas

Green Llamas is here to help you make all your People and Projects Green. Let us get your herds under control using the monday.com platform.

posts.author: Randi1 (Randi Fuchs)
posts.cookedHtml:
@mark.anley Thanks for getting back to me and explaining the complexity in making this update. My specific use case is around cleaning up mirrored fields that do not apply to that particular group.

In this example, we have a “Blog Publish Date” mirror column that feeds in from another board. This date is only helpful for one of the groups (Header/Blog Image) and not the others.

It would be helpful to hide the mirror column in the other groups. Let me know if you have a suggestion on how to clean this up!

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Randi1. A simple workaround to this would be to use a combination of filters and hidden columns. You could setup a filter that removes the group that is populated with this data, then hide the column entirely and save as a new board view.

Another idea, you could also setup a specific board just for this instance where the date is populated. You could then connect the multiple boards up into a single overview.

They are workarounds but may offer what you need to cleanup your board views.

Thanks
Mark

posts.author: inesam (Inês Mendes)
posts.cookedHtml:
Any news on the possibility of hiding columns in some groups? I don’t mean remove the columns completely, I understand it would be technically difficult to implement different columns for different groups in the same view as mentioned above.
But is restricting view of a column in just one group and no the rest being considered for the roadmap or not?

posts.author: jeyhun (Jeyhun Huseynov)
posts.cookedHtml:
Greetings!

There would not be a significant added implementation complexity to the by-group column hiding.

posts.author: asplundsoftware (Brad)
posts.cookedHtml:
I know this is quite an old request, but have you tried Column Groups - monday.com Marketplace ? It is aimed at solving this problem by letting you create custom vertical column groups within each item group separately.

In effect, this means you can make vertical groups for the columns you don’t want to see, within each horizontal item group, and then hide them so that you only see the data you care about in each section.

For example, you can go from this:

image1416×885 141 KB

To this:

image1416×885 128 KB

Let me know if this would work for your use case, or what could be better. I’m always looking for constructive feedback from real teams!

Thanks!

Cheers,
Brad
